Consider what makes this problem harder than Two Sum. It's the requirement to find unique triplets. To help identify uniqueness, what preprocessing step might be useful?
Try sorting the array first. Sorting makes it much easier to skip duplicate elements because they will be adjacent.

Once sorted, you can iterate through the array, fixing one element at index `i`. Then, the problem reduces to a Two Sum problem on the remaining elements from `i+1` to the end of the array, looking for a target of `-nums[i]`.

Since the remainder of the array is sorted, you can solve the Two Sum subproblem optimally using a Two Pointer approach (one pointer at `i+1`, the other at the end of the array), moving them inwards based on whether the sum is too small or too large.
